text.title: Service Ownership Map Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of service-ownership-map-template enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Every service has a named owning team — never 'shared' or 'TBD'; orphan services are resolved within 30 days.
rule.rationale: Shared ownership = no ownership; orphan services accumulate incidents and stall improvements.

rule.statement: Every service entry links to: on-call rotation, SLO doc, runbook, dependency list.
rule.rationale: Missing links turn the map into a phone book; the value is the linked artefacts.

rule.statement: Map is version-controlled (Backstage catalog-info.yaml OR Markdown in repo) — never a wiki page.
rule.rationale: Wiki pages drift; version-controlled maps diff like code.

rule.statement: Quarterly audit: every service entry verified against reality; stale entries removed; new services added.
rule.rationale: Unaudited maps decay to fiction in 6-12 months.

rule.statement: Dependency graph is computed (or curated) and surfaced as part of incident response — 'what depends on this service?'.
rule.rationale: Without the dependency view, every incident asks the same question.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, skip this methodology and route to the adjacent one referenced in Related.
rule.rationale: A skip rule keeps the decision tree closed: every branch ends at a real rule id.
